title: AITC Training Intern
description:
Join us in Transforming Lives Together!

Autism Resource Centre (Singapore) or ARC(S) is a non-profit charity organisation dedicated to serving individuals on the autism spectrum throughout their lives. Based in Singapore, ARC(S) offers a range of services and resources tailored to meet the growing concerns and needs of the autism community.

The team at Autism Intervention, Training & Consultancy (AITC) provides training and consultancy services for professionals and caregivers, as well as direct intervention services for individuals on the autism spectrum.

As an AITC Training Intern, your Impact and Contribution includes:

  • Assist with participant registration, enquiries, and general administrative tasks.
  • Prepare and consolidate participant lists for training events.
  • Liaise with sponsoring bodies, schools, trainers, and participants.
  • Support venue setup and day-of-event operations.
  • Edit, format, and prepare course outlines, handouts, and training materials.
  • Provide photography and videography support during events.
  • Perform data entry, filing, and documentation of training activities.
  • Assist with logistics coordination, including materials, equipment, and refreshments.
  • Support post-event tasks such as feedback collection and report preparation.
  • Help manage digital resources and organize media files.
  • Carry out general office duties and ad-hoc tasks as assigned.


Requirements

  • Good communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Basic proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Interest or basic skills in photography/videography is a plus.
  • Detail-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple tasks.
  • Ability to work on selected Saturdays when required.


Required competencies and certifications

  • Ability to work on selected Saturdays when required

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Good communication and interpersonal skills to engage effectively with stakeholders
  • Basic proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) to prepare documents and reports
  • Interest or basic skills in photography/videography to support event documentation
  • Detail-oriented and organized to manage multiple tasks efficiently


Other Information

Duration: 6 months

Working Hours: 8am to 5pm

Salary: $1400 per month

Benefits

Experience a supportive staff culture that nurtures wellbeing, bringing warmth and connection to your experience. Take the opportunity to build soft skills, deepen your understanding of neurodiversity, and gain insights that add a new dimension to your career journey.

We seek your understanding that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Our organisation is committed to protecting our children and young people from harm. We require all applicants to undergo a comprehensive screening process prior to hire.

postedCompany.description:
The Autism Resource Centre (Singapore) [ARC(S)], www.autism.org.sg, is one of the most active non-profit organizations in Singapore, dedicated to support individuals with autism and help them lead meaningful lives in society. ARC(S) runs an Early Intervention Unit, Pathlight School, Autism Training and Employability Training & Employment services.


In addition, Pathlight School, www.pathlight.org.sg  started by ARC(S) is the first autism-focused school offering a blend of Singapore mainstream academics and life skills training to students with autism and other related learning disorders.
postedCompany.name: Autism Resource Centre (Singapore)
